# adjustSelection(by:completionHandler:)

**Instance Method**

Adjusts the selection by the moving the selected range by the given `range`, in character granularity units.

## Declaration

```swift
func adjustSelection(by range: BEDirectionalTextRange, completionHandler: @escaping () -> Void)
```

```swift
func adjustSelection(by range: BEDirectionalTextRange) async
```

## Discussion

The start of the current selection is moved by `range.offset` characters, and the length of the selection is modified by `range.length` characters. For instance, if the current selection is the word “world” in “Hello world” and the `range` is `{ -6, -2 }`, the selected text after adjustment will be “Hel”.

## Selecting text

- **selectedText**: String representing the selected text.
- **selectedTextRange**: Range representing the selected text.
- **isSelectionAtDocumentStart**: Represents whether the current selection is at the beginning of the document
- **caretRect(for:)**: Returns a rectangle to draw the caret at a specified insertion point.
- **selectionRects(for:)**: Returns an array of selection rects corresponding to the range of text.
- **selectWordForReplacement()**: Selects a word with autocorrect replacement suggestions when it is tapped
- **updateSelection(extent:boundary:completionHandler:)**: Includes the text up to the given point in the current text selection.
- **selectText(in:at:completionHandler:)**: Selects the text within the given granularity at the given point in the text view.
- **selectPosition(at:completionHandler:)**: Sets the selection caret to the given point
- **selectPosition(at:for:completionHandler:)**: Sets the selection caret to the given point.  Also includes a convenience document context request.
- **move(byOffset:)**: Adjusts the current selection by `offset` in character granularity units
- **moveSelection(atBoundary:in:completionHandler:)**: Moves the caret to relative to the current position in the `direction` to the given `granularity`. The `direction` is “forward” or “backward” in accordance with the directionality of the language.
- **selectTextForEditMenuWithLocation(inView:completionHandler:)**: Indicates the edit menu is being shown at the given location in the text input view’s coordinate space.
